channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Nick Rimando elected to National Soccer Hall of Fame
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Former Real Salt Lake goalkeeper Nick Rimando has been elected to the National Soccer Hall of Fame. Rimando will be enshrined with the Class of 2025 on May 3, 2025 in Frisco, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sterling Sharpe and Mike Holmgren are picked as Hall of Fame finalists
item.description: Prolific receiver Sterling Sharpe and Super Bowl-winning coach Mike Holmgren have advanced to the final stage of voting for the 2025 Pro Football Hall of Fame class.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NFL's Goodell and Commanders' Harris meet with lawmakers to talk about potential RFK stadium site
item.description: NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell and Washington Commanders controlling owner Josh Harris have met with lawmakers on Capitol Hill as efforts push forward to secure access to the RFK Stadium site in the District of Columbia.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texans' Azeez Al-Shaair suspended 3 games without pay after violent hit on Trevor Lawrence
item.description: Houston’s Azeez Al-Shaair was suspended by the NFL without pay for three games for repeated violations of player safety rules following his hit to the head of Jacksonville’s Trevor Lawrence, which led to a concussion.
item.dc:creator: Kristie Rieken, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Matthews scores first goal since return from injury, Maple Leafs beat Blackhawks 4-1
item.description: Auston Matthews scored his first goal since returning from injury as the Toronto Maple Leafs defeated the Chicago Blackhawks 4-1 on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Caitlin Clark, Angel Reese open 2025 WNBA season against each other as schedule releases
item.description: The 2025 WNBA season schedule has been released. Caitlin Clark's Indiana Fever and Angel Reese's Chicago Sky begin their respective seasons against each other.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texans' Azeez Al-Shaair takes aim at 'racist and Islamophobic fans' in apology for hit on Trevor Lawrence
item.description: Houston Texans linebacker Azeez Al-Shaair took aim at "racist and Islamophobic" fans who criticized him for his hit on Jacksonville Jaguars quarterback Trevor Lawrence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz come up short against Lakers, 105-104
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Anthony Davis had 33 points and 11 rebounds, LeBron James added 27 points and 14 assists, and the short-handed Los Angeles Lakers beat the Utah Jazz 105-104 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Philadelphia defense, Tucker's misses help the Eagles to a 24-19 win in their showdown with Ravens
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Saquon Barkley vs. Derrick Henry was never supposed to be a head-to-head matchup. “It ain't basketball. It ain't like I go out there and tackle Derrick Henry,” Barkley said. “To be honest, I don't know if I really want to tackle Derrick Henry.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McLaughlin's field goal in OT helps Bucs beat Panthers 26-23, move into 1st-place tie in NFC South
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers beat the Carolina Panthers 26-23 in overtime on a 30-yard field goal by Chase McLaughlin on Sunday to move into a tie for first place in the NFC South.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mitchell scores 35, Cavs end Celtics' winning streak with 115-111 win in matchup of East's elite
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored 20 of his 35 points in the fourth quarter as the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Boston's seven-game winning streak with a 115-111 victory over the Celtics on Sunday night in the second intense matchup between the Eastern Conference’s top teams this season.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Montrose def. Pueblo West 18-7 to advance to the championship game
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The Montrose Red Hawks are heading to the Class 4A state championship game after defeating the Pueblo West Cyclones 18-7 in a hard-fought semifinal matchup.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Gus Malzahn is leaving UCF to become Florida State's offensive coordinator, AP source says
item.description: Gus Malzahn is resigning as Central Florida's head coach to become Florida State's offensive coordinator, a person familiar with the hire told The Associated Press on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers extend contract of postseason hero
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers have agreed to a contract extension with one of the key pieces of the team's World Series title run. The Dodgers reached agreement Friday on a deal with Tommy Edman, ensuring that the do-it-all utility-man will remain with the team for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, WGN 9

item.title: 49ers QB Brock Purdy remains severely limited by injury to his throwing shoulder
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Brock Purdy threw one short pass in the open portion of practice for the San Francisco 49ers as he remains slowed by an injury to his throwing shoulder that has already forced him to miss a game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Lions beat Bears 23-20 to extend win streak to 10 games
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Jared Goff threw two touchdown passes to Sam LaPorta, Jake Bates made three field goals in the first half and the Detroit Lions held off the Chicago Bears 23-20 on Thursday to extend their winning streak to 10 games. The Lions snapped a seven-game losing streak on Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jon Batiste, Ledisi, Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle to perform during Super Bowl pregame
item.description: The Super Bowl pregame will have some Louisiana flavor: Multi-talented performer Jon Batiste will hit the stage to sing the national anthem, while Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle are slated to perform “America the Beautiful.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Jose State is disappointed Boise State forfeited but looks forward to volleyball final
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — San Jose State said Thursday it was disappointed Boise State forfeited its semifinal match in the Mountain West women's volleyball tournament but that the Spartans were focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Real Salt Lake makes 2024 season-end roster decisions
item.description: Real Salt Lake has revealed its end-of-season roster decision following a record-setting 2024 campaign in Major League Soccer.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Wembanyama scores 34 as Spurs beat Jazz, 128-115
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - 17 games into the NBA season, the Utah Jazz and San Antonio Spurs have already played each other four times, and the Jazz have to be glad they don't have to see Victor Wembanyama again this season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Vucevic and White lead Bulls to 127-108 win over Wizards, losers of 12 straight
item.description: Nikola Vucevic scored 19 points and grabbed 12 rebounds as the Chicago Bulls eased to a 127-108 victory over the struggling Washington Wizards on Tuesday night in an NBA Cup game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
